birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Woodhouse's scrub jay (Aphelocoma woodhouseii) is a species of scrub jay native to western North America, ranging from southeastern Oregon and southern Idaho to central Mexico

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: During the breeding season, their range extends to the southern interior of British Columbia, as well as Wyoming, Montana, Idaho, and Utah. They are commonly found in rocky open habitats, such as exposed rock, canyons, cliffs; however, rock is not a firm requirement, as they can also be found in large eroded sandbanks. Rock wrens are rare in urban areas, instead preferring rocky areas with at least some steep features, enough shrubbery to forage in, and ample crevices for nesting. The rock wren is very tolerant of altitudes, breeding from to above sea level;{{Cite journal |last=Patten |first=Michael A

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: However, birds have been spotted far outside their range in such places as southern Alaska, Idaho, Saskatchewan, New York, Florida, Louisiana, and Newfoundland
